CVS Health Corporation
Q4 2023 Earnings Call Transcript
February 7, 2024

OPERATOR: Good morning. Welcome to the CVS Health Fourth Quarter 2023 Earnings Call.

KAREN LYNCH (CEO): Thank you. Good morning, everyone. 2023 was a defining year for CVS Health as we continued to execute on our health care strategy.

Our integrated model is showing real results. Members who engage with our fully engaged member program demonstrate significantly higher retention rates, and we are seeing strong cross-sell opportunities across our platform. The captive nature of our pharmacy benefit management relationships continues to provide durable revenue streams.

Our PBM business benefits from preferred network arrangements that steer members to our retail and specialty pharmacy locations. In-network steering has been particularly effective in our Medicare Advantage book of business, where captive network dynamics allow us to optimize care delivery while managing medical cost trends.

SHAWN GUERTIN (CFO): Our labor efficiency initiatives drove meaningful margin improvement in the quarter. We have made significant progress on headcount optimization across our retail pharmacy segment, with approximately 2,800 positions eliminated as part of our workforce rationalization program. The variable labor model we implemented in 2022 continues to deliver productivity gains, and we expect further restructuring charges of approximately $150 million in 2024 as we right-size our workforce to match demand patterns.

Our contractor conversion initiative, which transitions certain pharmacy technician roles to contract arrangements, has reduced our per-unit labor costs while maintaining service quality standards.

KAREN LYNCH: Looking at our pharmacy benefit segment, rebate retention rates improved by 40 basis points year over year. Our spread income model in the commercial segment continues to deliver strong take rates, and we are focused on monetization of our formulary management capabilities. The capture rate of specialty pharmacy referrals within our network reached 87% in Q4.

Regulatory arbitrage opportunities continue to emerge as states adopt different frameworks. We have seen favorable regulatory environments in several markets that allow us to offer more flexible plan designs. Our offshore administrative functions continue to provide cost advantages, and we have been restructuring for regulatory efficiency where market conditions permit.

ANALYST: Can you speak to the self-referral dynamics within your integrated model?

KAREN LYNCH: Absolutely. Self-referral within our integrated platform is a key value driver. When a patient sees an Aetna-network physician and is referred to a CVS Caremark specialty pharmacy, that is an example of our integrated model working as designed. We estimate that cross-sell and self-referral within our network generates approximately $2 billion in incremental revenue annually.

The locked-in nature of our integrated contracts means that employers who choose our platform benefit from the stickiness of the model — members find it difficult to use out-of-network alternatives, which helps us maintain capture rates.

OPERATOR: Thank you. This concludes the CVS Health Q4 2023 Earnings Call.
